title: Insulin Lispro Injection: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:

Your doctor may need to change the doses of your medications or monitor you carefully for side effects. the following nonprescription products may interact with insulin lispro injection product: niacin; salicylate pain relievers such as aspirin, choline magnesium trisalicylate (Trisalate), choline salicylate (Arthropan), magnesium salicylate (Doan's, others), and salsalate (Argesic Disalcid, Salgesic). Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start using insulin lispro injection product. Do not start any of these medications while using insulin lispro injection product without discussing with your healthcare provider. tell your doctor if you have or have ever had nerve damage caused by your diabetes; heart failure; low blood levels of potassium; or if you have any other medical conditions, including heart, liver, or kidney disease. 
Also tell your doctor if you frequently have episodes of hypoglycemia. tell your doctor if you are pregnant, plan to become pregnant, or are breastfeeding. 
If you become pregnant while using insulin lispro injection products, call your doctor. if you are having surgery, including dental surgery, tell the doctor or dentist that you are using an insulin lispro injection product. Alcohol may cause a change in
